entry.title: Washington Post: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: First grain shipment leaves Odessa; Black Sea port Mykolaiv hit in ‘brutal’ shelling
  • As Putin squeezes gas supplies, Germany is falling back on coal
  • First ship carrying grain leaves Odessa in deal to ease global food crisis
  • Kosovo-Serbia tensions flare; NATO peacekeepers track border protests
  • Russia’s war in Ukraine finds echoes in the Balkans
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: ‘Brutal shellings’ rock port city and kill one of Ukraine’s richest businessmen
  • Former Philippine president Fidel Ramos dies at 94
  • Pelosi announces Asia trip itinerary with no mention of Taiwan
  • China’s most powerful rocket falls back to Earth, lands in criticism
  • Threat of protests, violent escalation stirs panic in Iraq
  • UK court holds last-minute hearing in life support battle
  • Kosovo delays measure stirring tensions with minority Serbs
  • Australian Parliament considers lifting euthanasia ban
  • Blaze in southern France, which injured 4, contained
  • 2 years later, hope for justice in Beirut explosion fades
  • No reported damage in Philippines from Chinese rocket debris
  • Ramos, ex-Philippine leader who helped oust dictator, dies
  • Salvadoran Archbishop praises government crackdown on gangs
  • Drone explosion hits Russia's Black Sea Fleet headquarters
  • McKinney Fire is now California’s largest wildfire this year
  • Man with assault rifle arrested near Iranian American writer’s Brooklyn home
  • Death toll for Kentucky floods climbs to 28, with more storms coming
  • Nichelle Nichols, who played Uhura in ‘Star Trek’ franchise, dies at 89
  • An Oklahoma city’s first openly gay mayor resigned. Then came the fallout.
  • Fires engulf western Nebraska wildlife area, sparking evacuations
  • Post Politics Now: Senate Democrats seeking a big win by week’s end
  • Biden covid case highlights confusing CDC guidance on ending isolation
  • A policy win, an economic hit: Turbulent week reflects Biden’s challenge
  • Thousands of lives depend on a transplant network in need of ‘vast restructuring’
  • What to do if sellers don’t give you all the house keys at closing
  • Two-bedroom, two-bathroom condo in Bowie, Md., lists for $215,000
  • The Bank of England Needs a Big Mac and Fries
  • Wishful Thinking Won’t Help the Fed Beat Inflation
  • Why Stocks Took a July Vacay From Fundamentals
  • Charter-School Change Is a Victory for Children
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • What to Know About Bolsonaro-Lula Showdown in Brazil
  • What the Hong Kong Dollar Peg Is and Why It Matters
  • What’s Driving the US-China Spat Over Audits and Delisting
  • ‘Nope’ director Jordan Peele mocks his audience. They love him for it.
  • Want to know what a ‘banana republic’ looks like?
  • Cartoon by Drew Sheneman
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 1 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-01 11:22AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • AFL round 20 key takeouts and match review news
  • Best of cartoons, August 2, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox

  • No go ahead for De Goey from Saints board... yet
  • Home Affairs warns of online movements ‘fracturing’ Australian society
  • Tourism groups urge action on short-stay properties
  • Reserve Bank poised for historic fourth consecutive hike
  • What’s an Indigenous Voice to parliament? What are the steps to make it happen?
  • Aged care worker registration delayed until next year
  • Dutton readies for fight to keep $2.9b fuel excuse cut going
  • Thorpe’s ‘coloniser’ Queen oath out of order as former leaders find backbench
  • Farmers urge tax break for pensioners to unleash grey army of fruit pickers
  • Best of cartoons, August 1, 2022
  • Labor puts gas companies on notice as supply shortfall looms
  • Aged care minister reflects on ‘huge’ responsibility to fix the sector she once worked in
  • Small step or giant leap?: What happened when the WA Liberals looked in the mirror
  • Child poverty: A problem Labor can and must solve
  • Union head demands default membership for skilled migrants
  • Spender calls for tax reform while delivering a warning to the Coalition
  • Parliament to host de facto euthanasia debate with territory rights bill
  • House values tumble and could drop faster as rates set to rise
  • Bipartisan support for Labor’s climate plans crucial, green groups and industry say
  • Wounded pride: Is Sydney ready to host the biggest LGBTQ+ event?
  • Swift sacking of minister ahead of landmark review of NSW parliament culture
  • ‘Timid and upset’: Mystery deepens over Saudi sisters’ deaths
  • For the sake of NSW, Stuart Ayres must resign
  • Murder admission over death of car wrecking business owner was a lie, jury told
  • Stuart MacGill had money problems, court told, as alleged kidnappers seek bail
  • Brawl breaks out outside Leichhardt Oval after Sydney FC soccer match
  • Guy Sebastian’s ex-manager awaits decision on third attempt to detain him
  • Push for admin fees to be slashed as part of review of Sydney tolls
  • Seals visiting NSW
  • ‘Manhattan without Central Park’: The Sydney suburb fighting for green space
  • Man stabbed during brutal home invasion in Sydney’s west
  • Sydney’s mega rail projects running into ‘high’ safety, financial risks
  • Teachers to get helping hand preparing for lessons
  • ‘Totally out of scale’: Towers set to rise above 100 metres in Pyrmont
  • Enact Voice so we never again overlook Indigenous
  • In the Herald: August 1, 1936
  • ‘Very serious interest’: Indonesia wants AUKUS submarines monitored by UN watchdog
  • School students help overturn 329 year old conviction for last Salem ‘witch’
  • Ukrainian grain ship departs Odesa for the first time since Russian invasion
  • ‘Who blinks first’: Pelosi begins Asia trip, China starts military exercises
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • World’s biggest yacht stranded as Dutch city turns on Jeff Bezos, upending plan to dismantle iconic bridge
  • Drone ‘terror’ attack hits Russia fleet headquarters, 170km from Ukraine mainland
  • Earth records shortest day, putting clocks (slightly) out of time
  • Ardern says New Zealand will co-operate with China on ‘shared interests’
  • Beirut silo collapses, reviving trauma as blast anniversary nears
  • Nichelle Nichols, groundbreaking Star Trek actress, is dead at 89
  • Fidel Ramos, ex-Philippine leader who helped oust dictator, dies
  • How masks on some of Victoria’s youngest could save our oldest
  • I was a banker, now I’m on welfare - I had no idea how hard it would be
  • Australia news LIVE: PM says Indigenous Voice detail to come after referendum; fresh calls to work from home as COVID winter wave continues
  • Lawyers offer $2000 reward for keepsake stolen by jewellery thief
  • Global malt supplier shrugs off share price slide
  • Gloves are off: Government versus the gas giants
  • The Wrap: ASX edges higher to kick off August in style
  • NAB chairman Phil Chronican points to risks of financing new gas projects
  • Afterpay warns of spike in scam texts ‘across the financial services industry’
  • Billionaire Scott Farquhar’s bid for renewable energy firm rejected
  • Brain-dead: China’s embattled property giant on road to nowhere
  • ‘There’s value there’: GQG Partners defends funds managers amid passive investment surge
  • Game on: Bids roll in for multi-billion dollar AFL deal
  • ‘Supermarkets will do well’ as grocery prices rise
  • Mark Ryan exits embattled journalism institute
  • ‘It will be really positive’: ABC news boss to work in Parramatta when staff relocate
  • The inflation fix: Protect profits, and hit workers and consumers
  • Why is everyone mad at celebrities flying private jets?
  • Yes, Hunted is enjoyably stupid TV ... and I’ve watched every minute
  • ‘We thought they’d hate it and punch us,’ says Hairspray creator
  • In this face-off between old painters and new, there’s a surprise knockout
  • The top five gigs you shouldn’t miss in Sydney in August
  • Start practising the Nutbush: The Tina Turner Musical is heading to Sydney
  • New artistic director appointed for Sydney Writers’ Festival
  • Serial killers with a fin: ‘Sharknado effect’ produces a surge in killer shark films
  • ‘Whatever you do, make it count’: Kerry O’Brien’s advice to Stan Grant
  • Selfie-indulgent athletes: Please hop, step or jump away from your phones
  • Climate, inflation hang over Diggers and Dealers forum
  • Failure to spend bushfire donations prompts WIRES board defection
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Daily round-up, Aug 1: 60 per cent of Singapore residents have had COVID-19; vaccination boosters for children; Malaysia lifts chicken export ban

  • Tech firm W3BCLOUD to go public via $1.25 billion SPAC deal

  • Bankrupt Sri Lanka seeks urgent help to feed children

  • Indonesia to issue nickel export tax rules in Q3 -official

  • COVID-19 vaccination booster shots for children aged 5 to 11 to be rolled out in about 2 months: MOH
  • Lawyer Charles Yeo seeking ‘political asylum’ in the UK
  • 60% of Singapore residents have caught COVID-19, but this doesn't mean there is herd immunity: Ong Ye Kung
  • SkillsFuture Singapore has contacted all parties to recover S$4.22 million in grant overpayments: MOE
  • Leong Mun Wai files petition in Parliament calling for temporary suspension of SERS
  • Social gambling among family, friends at home legalised from Aug 1 as new laws take effect

entry.title: Guardian: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England win Women’s Euro 2022 final: players and fans celebrate in London – live!
  • Commonwealth Games 2022: hockey, cycling and Peaty back in the pool on day four – live!
  • England’s Euros triumph draws record TV audience of 17m
  • British man killed and six people injured in Sardinia yacht crash
  • Premier League 2022-23 preview No 12: Liverpool
  • The people making a difference: the woman who created a community for fellow adoptees
  • Hotter than Dubai: US cities at risk of Middle Eastern temperatures by 2100
  • Break stuff! How Limp Bizkit, rioting fans and a huge candle handout led to a music festival fiasco

  • UK farmers count cost as heatwave kills fruit and vegetable crops
  • Eurozone factory activity contracts as recession fears rise; China’s home sales slump – business live

  • Six Queensland police officers accused of domestic violence within two weeks, inquiry told
  • Nichelle Nichols obituary
  • Australia holding immigration detainees for average of almost two years, freedom of information request reveals
  • Grain ship leaves Ukraine port for first time since Russia blockade

  • Anti-vax Twitter accounts pushing food crisis misinformation, study finds
  • Rachel Roddy’s recipe for crab linguine with lemon and herbs
  • How we met: ‘We got chatting at a comic-book convention and it turned into a naughty weekend’
  • Some babies sleep well, some don’t – beware those selling easy fixes | Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
  • England’s Toone urges fans now in love with women’s football to stick around
  • ‘At best it is brain-rotting’: Love Island will be banned in 50 years
  • Adam Gemili splits with under-investigation coach Rana Reider
  • Pitch perfect: 10 things to celebrate about Euro 2022
  • US’s proposed swap for Griner and Whelan met with skepticism and fury
  • Yes we can! Music, comedy and books to inspire confidence
  • ‘I’m doing puzzles that may take 10 years to solve’: Animal Well, a mysterious video game time capsule
  • Rishi Sunak rejects claim he has ‘doomster’ attitude to economy
  • Taiwan’s Rainbow Village defaced after operators told to move out
  • Australian senator calls the Queen a coloniser while being sworn in to parliament
  • Controversy, but louder: Stenson’s LIV Golf title takes backseat in Trumpland
  • How can England build on their Euro 2022 triumph?
  • Kelly’s winner sparks tears of joy everywhere and a wild sense of liberation | Sid Lowe
  • Four joyous girls in Harlem: how Lorraine O’Grady showed that art is for everyone
  • Alarm as Arizona Republicans set to nominate election deniers for top posts
  • Single servings at low prices: how Unilever’s sachets became an environmental scourge
  • When Beyoncé dropped the same ableist slur as Lizzo on her new album, my heart sank | Hannah Diviney
  • The climate crisis is so boring – but I also hate the idea of burning to death | Sofie Hagen
  • Only a country as complacent as the UK could give up its border privilege so easily | Nesrine Malik
  • ‘My karaoke song? Pure & Simple by Hear’Say, because I wrote it!’: Betty Boo’s honest playlist
  • New Zealand pornography producer pleads guilty to sex trafficking in US
  • UK businesses slash investments due to soaring prices and Brexit
  • ‘The establishment didn’t know what to do with me’: Sanjeev Bhaskar on marriage, success and stereotypes
  • ‘How many litres of blood do we need?’: Ivo van Hove’s ITA on 20 years of shocking theatregoers
  • Taliban policies risk de facto university ban for Afghan women, say officials
  • Nancy Pelosi begins Asia trip but does not mention Taiwan
  • New Zealand will continue to cooperate with ‘more assertive’ China, Ardern says
  • Kosovo delays plan for volatile north as tensions rise near Serbian border
  • McKinney fire grows into California’s largest this year as thunderstorms fuel concerns
  • Cristiano Ronaldo leaves Old Trafford before end of Manchester United friendly
  • Bill Russell, NBA superstar and civil rights activist, dies aged 88
  • The Lionesses have done it. This Euros win will change women’s football for ever | Carrie Dunn
  • Annemiek van Vleuten claims Tour de France Femmes despite six bike changes
  • Kentucky grapples with effect of climate crisis as floods leave trail of devastation
  • Max Verstappen pulls away in F1 standings after victory at Hungarian GP
  • Ukraine’s harvest could be halved this year due to Russian invasion, warns Zelenskiy
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Korea Herald: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 1 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-01 11:03AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong Observatory says July was city’s hottest month on record
  • Hong Kong must have a plan to end quarantine if it is to keep international financial hub status, retain talent, fund managers say
  • Hong Kong’s graft-buster charges senior fire services officer with fraud for allegedly swindling bank, corporation out of HK$7.8 million
  • Hong Kong slips into recession as economy shrinks by 1.4 per cent in second quarter
  • Coronavirus: 22-month-old girl dies from Covid-19 in Hong Kong after falling ill last week
  • People’s Liberation Army’s Hong Kong garrison holds first flag-raising ceremony at Central designated zone marking military anniversary
  • Mirror concert accident: band breaks silence to rally support for dancer fighting for life in Hong Kong
  • How much has remote learning damaged Hong Kong students and how quickly can the damage be undone? Teachers talk about the harm and how they’re helping the healing
  • Furniture from toppled trees: Hong Kong ‘yard waste’ recycling centre turns discards into new products
  • Marry, have a baby? No thanks, say more Hongkongers, as experts blame pandemic, cost of living, sky-high home prices
  • Snoozing Hong Kong police officer faces disciplinary action after service gun left unattended
  • Coronavirus: no special quarantine deal for critically hurt Mirror dancer’s parents, Hong Kong officials say, with nearly 1,700 applications approved to leave isolation
  • China releases plan to guide carbon-intensive industries to reach peak emissions by 2030
  • China’s Communist Party mouthpiece launches NFT research project to ‘guide’ digital art development
  • ‘Lightning carrier concept’: lessons for China in US Navy’s use of amphibious ships as small carriers
  • China finds small is beautiful for African projects under belt and road
  • China’s foreign businesses adapt by looking inward for talent as zero-Covid keeps expats away
  • China’s capital market creaks open wider with ‘gamechanger’ law to slash transaction costs for over-the-counter derivatives
  • China’s factory activity grows at slower pace in July as output, new orders and employment soften
  • For America’s next generation of China experts, the challenges go beyond language and country access
  • What US-China game of chicken over Pelosi and Taiwan means for Southeast Asia
  • China’s Guangdong province rules out special health code for unvaccinated and warns against ‘excessive’ restrictions
  • China’s cyberspace watchdog reports ‘significant results’ in online platform regulation
  • Xi Jinping urges Chinese Communist Party to step up efforts to ‘win hearts and minds’ in Hong Kong and Taiwan
  • Singapore says stable US-China ties crucial as Nancy Pelosi keeps mum over Taiwan stop
  • Asia’s richest men prep to battle in India as Gautam Adani enters Mukesh Ambani’s turf
  • US military wants to expand ties with New Zealand, says Pacific commander who called China ‘destabilising’
  • Singaporeans ‘don’t want’ gay marriage, law minister says, as he signals Section 377A review under way
  • Rescued Australia turtle pooped plastic for 6 days: ‘Luck was on his side, he excreted all of it out’
  • Russia’s Singapore envoy says image in Asia remains ‘snow-white’, Moscow has ‘no regrets’ after Ukraine invasion
  • China urged to condemn Russia by New Zealand’s Ardern: ‘Our differences need not define us’
  • Myanmar military government extends state of emergency for another 6 months
  • Vietnam robber-cop arrested after offering passers-by gold and jewellery he stole in raid on shops
  • Philippines’ ex-president Fidel Ramos, who helped end Marcos dictatorship, dies at 94
  • Iran nuclear chief: we have technical means to produce atomic bomb, but have no intention of doing so
  • ‘Relief for world’ as first Ukraine grain ship since start of war departs Odesa
  • ‘Living with Covid-19’: where the pandemic could go next
  • Ukraine-Russia war hangs over UN meeting on nuclear weapons
  • Truss vs Sunak: Divisive Margaret Thatcher looms over UK leadership race
  • Part of Beirut silo collapses, reviving trauma ahead of blast anniversary
  • UK brings in ownership register for property held by foreign companies
  • Kentucky flood death toll hits 28 with more bodies expected ‘for weeks’
  • UK PM race: Rishi Sunak vows 20 per cent income tax cut by 2029
  • Nichelle Nichols, Star Trek’s trailblazing Lieutenant Uhura, dies at 89
  • England’s Lionesses roar to victory, beating Germany 2-1 at Euro 2022 final
  • Influential Iraqi cleric urges more to join parliament takeover
  • Israel’s prime minister vows investigation into ‘sex slave’ prison case
  • British geologist jailed in Iraq for smuggling antiquities freed after conviction quashed
  • Putin says Russian Navy will soon have hypersonic missiles five times faster than the speed of sound
  • Commonwealth Games: Horrifying crash injures cyclists and spectators
  • Ukrainian grain tycoon killed as first export ship prepares to leave Odesa
  • Prince Charles’ charity accepted £1million from Bin Laden family
  • CSRC, China’s securities watchdog, pledges to prevent ‘abnormal fluctuations’ in embattled stock market
  • Chinese studio Bona Film, maker of Battle at Lake Changjin, set for Shenzhen IPO after Nasdaq departure
  • China’s new home prices in July record first decline in eight months as mortgage-boycott crisis weighs on sentiment
  • Carry traders are using the weakening euro to score major wins in the emerging markets
  • HSBC posts second-quarter surprise as profit jumps unexpectedly by 61 per cent on deferred tax gain, helping bank to beat estimates
  • China is not going to bail out its property sector, despite investors’ wishful thinking
  • Hong Kong stocks rebound from 11-week low on HSBC earnings surprise while Alibaba, China manufacturing data weigh on market
  • Thousands of Shanghai’s small businesses struggle to survive as virus curbs remain a stumbling block
  • Warning shot from China’s earnings season for stock bulls as UBS, JPMorgan see bumpy second-half ride
  • Signs of US economic slowdown complicate Federal Reserve’s inflation battle
  • Multiple wedding dresses for Meghan, Jennifer Lopez, Britney Spears – more people are saying yes to two or more outfits for their big day
  • Affordable Art Fair Hong Kong 2022 focuses on young artists from the city and local art galleries, many of them recently opened
  • What’s Buckingham Palace like? Inside the British royals’ State Rooms, as tours open for the first time since 2019
  • K-drama midseason recap: Extraordinary Attorney Woo – Netflix drama series about a lawyer with autism will give you all the feels
  • Urban farming a form of mental health therapy for rooftop gardeners in Hong Kong, and it fosters a sense of community and healthy eating, advocates say
  • Solar panels designed with attractive patterns to win over Nimbys who don’t like industrial look, and 5 installations, from Singapore to Dubai, that showcase elegance
  • Why it’s easier now to buy a rare Rolex Daytona or Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711 – and cheaper, too
  • Durian on pizza, in curry, quiche and chicken soup – chefs in Hong Kong serve pungent ‘king of fruits’ in savoury dishes
  • Disney+ K-drama Adamas: Ji Sung plays double roles in stylish high-society mystery
  • Yes, the Aurora Diamante fountain pen costs US$1.47 million – here’s why, plus 3 more super-fancy writing pieces from Cartier, Montegrappa and Mont Blanc
  • Inside Anya Hindmarch’s sustainable fashion revolution: after the iconic ‘I am not a plastic bag’ with celebrity fans like Keira Knightley and Reese Witherspoon, she’s back with The Universal Bag
  • 5 stars who made a comeback after retiring: from Cameron Diaz announcing a new Netflix film and Tom Brady returning to football after just 40 days, to Jay-Z being ‘terrible’ at retirement
  • Meet Formula One heiress Tamara Ecclestone, daughter of billionaire Bernie Ecclestone: she’s offering millions for her stolen jewellery – and lives in a mansion near Prince William and Kate Middleton
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Deutsche Bank broke its own rules in enabling tax fraud, internal probe finds
  • Venture capital’s silent crash: when the tech boom met reality
  • The triple whammy for office real estate
  • Biden’s surprise win will bring economic as well as political benefits
  • European shares subdued after July rebound for global stocks
  • Ukraine’s first grain ship sails after Russia relaxes Black Sea blockade
  • Shock jock diplomacy: Ukraine’s gadfly ambassador bids farewell to Berlin
  • Hong Kong falls into second recession in 3 years as coronavirus restrictions sting
  • Wave of corporate exits sends chill through Chicago’s business elite
  • Life under the Taliban: ‘what matters is that we’re hungry’
  • Politics in the workplace: how should we deal with opposing views?
  • Alibaba to ‘strive’ to keep New York listing despite addition to SEC watchlist
  • Chipmakers battle for slice of US government support
  • Manchin says climate bill closes ‘loopholes’ but does not raise taxes
  • India to surpass China as world’s biggest buyer of minerals
  • HSBC pledges to restore dividend to pre-pandemic levels
  • Heineken sales jump as drinkers soak up higher prices
  • Serie A head warns foreign buyers not to put short-term profit over long-term investment
  • England crowned Euro 2022 champions with 2-1 win over Germany
  • Rishi Sunak accuses Liz Truss of clinging to failed Treasury policies
  • Christian Candy sells ‘Candyland’ estate for £125mn
  • Poland’s mortgage holiday for households threatens bank profits
  • Bank of England considers biggest rate rise for more than 25 years
  • How foreign money is still fuelling abuses in Xinjiang
  • FirstFT: China steps up warnings as Pelosi begins Asia trip
  • Tracking Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in maps
  • Eataly CEO Nicola Farinetti’s favourite restaurants in Rome
  • Another Russia podcast finds a fresh angle on a familiar story — review
  • The UK has an energy security problem but does not want to admit it
  • FDA halts UK group’s baby formula imports over ‘modified’ paperwork
  • Tom Tugendhat: ‘I will serve in whatever capacity I’m asked to serve’
  • Lab space shortage threatens life science boom in Oxford and Cambridge
  • Has tighter monetary policy slowed the pace of US hiring?
  • Cracks in office property could cause structural damage
  • Advertising: digital slowdown creates opportunity for trad ad agencies
  • Blue Whale dumps Faang stocks on inflation fears
  • Reading the rally
  • Robust growth in Asia ETFs looks set to continue
  • Starling Bank faces questions over reliance on state-backed funding
  • Investors pressure UV protective gear maker Beneunder to downsize HK listing
  • West eases efforts to restrict Russian oil trading as inflation and energy risks mount
  • The science of feedback — FT readers respond
  • It’s time to talk about the menopause . . .
  • Boosting civic trust is essential to Latin American economic growth

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russian’s violin that survived the A-bomb touches Ukrainians’ souls
  • Olympic exec allegedly took a cut from Aoki’s sponsorship fees
  • Kishida hints he may reclassify COVID-19 into a lower category
  • Actress’s love of sumo pays off big time with post on yokozuna council

  • VOX POPULI: Politicians’ ties with Unification Church need to be scrutinized
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
